steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The poem describes a sail and sailor waiting for the west wind before they can move. The blank replaces 'idle' — find the word that best captures that state of non-activity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Inactive': means not in operation or doing nothing. A sail and sailor waiting for wind are inactive — not yet moving. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Unsupported': means lacking backing or structural support. Nothing in the poem suggests the sail lacks support. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Ridiculous': means absurd or worthy of mockery. The poem conveys patient waiting, not absurdity. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Untrustworthy': means unreliable. The poem makes no judgment about the sail's dependability. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
